title: Cassia pittieri
taxonomy: Cassia pittieri is classified in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the legume or pea family. This classification places Cassia pittieri in the tribe Cassieae, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Cassia is a part of the subfamily Caesalpinioideae, which contains about 850 species of flowering plants. Cassia pittieri is a species of flowering plant, and its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Fabaceae family. Further classification of Cassia pittieri may also include its specific epithet and taxonomic rank within the family Fabaceae.

intro: Cassia pittieri is a species of flowering plant in the legume family, Fabaceae, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems.
